Agaricus bisporus (cultivated mushroom) genome assembly, gfAgaBisp1
Agaricus bisporus (cultivated mushroom)
Darwin Tree of Life Project: Genome Data and Assemblies
Sanger Institute Tree of Life Programme: Data and Assemblies

Earth BioGenome Project (EBP)

assembly_info.comments:
The assembly gfAgaBisp1.1 is based on 21x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project
(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, retained haplotig separation with purge_dups, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using rapid curation. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. A TTAGGGGG telomeric motif was found and used to identify chromosomes.

bioprojects.title: Agaricus bisporus (cultivated mushroom) genome assembly, gfAgaBisp1, alternate haplotype

Earth BioGenome Project (EBP)

assembly_info.comments:
The assembly gfAgaBisp1.1 is based on 21x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project
(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, retained haplotig separation with purge_dups, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using rapid curation. This alternate haplotype assembly combines the haplotigs separated by purge_dups with haplotigs separated from the primary assembly during curation. A TTAGGGGG telomeric motif was found and used to identify chromosomes.

bioprojects.title: Agaricus bisporus var. bisporus Genome sequencing

assembly_info.comments:
URL -- http://genome.jgi.doe.gov/Agabi_varbisH97_2
JGI Project ID: 99190
The DNA was provided by Anton Sonnenberg(anton.sonnenberg@wur.nl)
The strain is available from Fungal Genetics Stock Center
Contacts: Igor V. Grigoriev (ivgrigoriev@lbl.gov)
Assembly and annotation done by JGI.
The JGI and collaborators endorse the principles for the distribution and use of large scale sequencing data adopted by the larger genome sequencing community and urge users of this data to follow them. It is our intention to publish the work of this project in a timely fashion and we welcome collaborative interaction on the project and analysis.
(http://www.genome.gov/page.cfm?pageID=10506376)
